recommendations.title: The Party 2
recommendations.overview: A young French teenage girl after moving to a new city falls in love with a boy and is thinking of having sex with him because her girlfriends have already done it.

recommendations.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
recommendations.overview: Against all the odds, a thirteen year old boy in Malawi invents an unconventional way to save his family and village from famine.

recommendations.title: Life in a Year
recommendations.overview: A 17 year old finds out that his girlfriend is dying, so he sets out to give her an entire life, in the last year she has left.

recommendations.title: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret.
recommendations.overview: When her family moves from New York City to New Jersey, an 11-year-old girl navigates new friends, feelings, and the beginning of adolescence.

recommendations.title: Badhaai Ho
recommendations.overview: A 25-year-old man tries to suppress his embarrassment when his mother announces that she is pregnant.

recommendations.title: Family Weekend
recommendations.overview: A 16-year-old girl takes her parents hostage after they miss her big jump-roping competition.

recommendations.title: Pauline at the Beach
recommendations.overview: Marion is about to divorce from her husband and takes her 15-year-old niece, Pauline, on a vacation to Granville. There, she meets an old love...

recommendations.title: Fireheart
recommendations.overview: The film explores the world of firefighters in 1920s New York City and tells the story of a 16-year-old girl who will have to become a hero in order to save her city.
recommendations.director: Laurent Zeitoun, Theodore Anthony Lee Ty

recommendations.title: Turning Red
recommendations.overview: Thirteen-year-old Mei is experiencing the awkwardness of being a teenager with a twist – when she gets too excited, she transforms into a giant red panda.

recommendations.title: Eighth Grade
recommendations.overview: Thirteen-year-old Kayla endures the tidal wave of contemporary suburban adolescence as she makes her way through the last week of middle school — the end of her thus far disastrous eighth grade year — before she begins high school.
